sourceSummary.oneLine: International Certification Corp. CE RF exposure report EA740701-11 for Laird Connectivity ST60-2230C 802.11 ac/a/b/g/n M.2 2230 + Bluetooth 4.2 module variants.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Use as evidence that the listed ST60-2230C module variants were evaluated for CE RF exposure against EN IEC 62311:2020, EN 50665:2017, and EN 50385:2017, with all recorded RF exposure evaluation results marked Pass.
keyPoints: Report No. EA740701-11, Report Version Rev. 01, initial issue Feb. 25, 2021.
Applicant is Laird Connectivity, Inc.; brand name is Laird Connectivity.
The report is supplementary to original ICC report nos. EA740701-08 and EA740701-06.
Model ST60-2230C was selected as representative for final test and only its data was recorded.
RF exposure evaluation limits reference general public exposure in Council Recommendation 1999/519/EC.
Evaluation results list all frequency ranges as Pass against a 61 V/m limit.

entities.name: International Certification Corp.
entities.evidence: We, International Certification Corp., would like to declare that the tested sample has been evaluated and in compliance with the requirement of the above standards.

entities.name: James Fan
entities.evidence: Reviewed by: James Fan / Assistant Manager

concepts.name: Council Recommendation 1999/519/EC
concepts.evidence: The device shall comply with the relevant limits for general public exposure specified as basic restrictions or reference levels in the Council Recommendation 1999/519/EC.

concepts.name: Far-field E-field strength evaluation
concepts.evidence: Follow below formula to evaluate E-field strength.

evidence.claim: Report EA740701-11 is a CE RF Exposure Report, Rev. 01.
evidence.evidenceText:
Report No.: EA740701-11 Page : 1 of 9
Report Version: Rev. 01
CE RF Exposure Report

evidence.claim: The equipment covered is an 802.11 ac/a/b/g/n M.2 2230 + Bluetooth 4.2 module, model ST60-2230C.
evidence.evidenceText:
Equipment : 802.11 ac/a/b/g/n M.2 2230 + Bluetooth 4.2 module
Model No. : ST60-2230C

evidence.claim: The applicant is Laird Connectivity, Inc. and the brand name is Laird Connectivity.
evidence.evidenceText:
Brand Name : Laird Connectivity
Applicant : Laird Connectivity, Inc.

evidence.claim: The report applies EN IEC 62311:2020, EN 50665:2017, and EN 50385:2017.
The report was initially issued on Feb. 25, 2021.
evidence.evidenceText:
Release Record
Report No. Version Description Issued Date
EA740701-11 Rev. 01 Initial issue Feb. 25, 2021

evidence.claim: The report is supplementary to ICC report nos. EA740701-08 and EA740701-06.
evidence.evidenceText: This report is issued as a supplementary report to original ICC report no. EA740701-08 and EA740701-06.

evidence.claim: The supplementary changes include additional models and product names, changing applicant and brand name, CONFIG_HOST resistance differences, and updated standard version.
evidence.evidenceText:
The modification is
1. Additional model and product names, changing applicant and brand name
2. Resistance of CONFIG_HOST0/1/2 are different to switch interface
3. Updating standard version

evidence.claim: The report states that the listed changes do not affect test items and all test results remain unchanged.
evidence.evidenceText: Above changes do not affect any test items, thus all test results remain unchanged.

evidence.claim: The product detail table lists ST60-2230C, ST60-2230C-P, ST60-2230C-PU, ST60-2230C-SS, ST60-2230C-U, ST60-2230C-UU, and ST60-2230C-Q.
evidence.evidenceText:
The following models are provided to this EUT.
Brand Name Model Name Product Name Description
Laird Connectivity
ST60-2230C
ST60-2230C-P
ST60-2230C-PU
ST60-2230C-SS
ST60-2230C-U
ST60-2230C-UU
ST60-2230C-Q

evidence.claim: Model ST60-2230C was selected as the representative model for the final test.
evidence.evidenceText: The above models, model ST60-2230C was selected as a representative one for the final test and only its data was recorded in this report.

evidence.claim: The WLAN operating frequencies include 2412-2472 MHz and 5180-5700 MHz ranges, and Bluetooth operating frequency is 2402-2480 MHz.
evidence.evidenceText:
WLAN
Operating Frequency
802.11b/g/n: 2412 MHz ~ 2472 MHz
802.11a/n/ac: 5180 MHz ~ 5240 MHz; 5260 MHz ~ 5320 MHz;
5500 MHz ~ 5700 MHz
BT
Operating Frequency 2402 MHz ~ 2480 MHz

evidence.claim: The EUT power supply type is DC 3.3V from host.
evidence.evidenceText:
1.1.4 EUT Operational Condition
Power Supply Type DC 3.3V from host

evidence.claim: The RF exposure limits section references general public exposure limits in Council Recommendation 1999/519/EC.
evidence.evidenceText: The device shall comply with the relevant limits for general public exposure specified as basic restrictions or reference levels in the Council Recommendation 1999/519/EC as below table.

evidence.claim: The report states there was no deviation from the test standard and measurement procedure.
evidence.evidenceText:
2.3 Deviation from Test Standard and Measurement Procedure
None

evidence.claim: Conducted power measurement uncertainty is ±0.808 dB.
All RF exposure evaluation rows are marked Pass against a 61 V/m limit.
International Certification Corp. states the tested sample was evaluated and in compliance with the above standards.
evidence.evidenceText: We, International Certification Corp., would like to declare that the tested sample has been evaluated and in compliance with the requirement of the above standards.

reviewFlags.reason: No external certificate or regulator-issued approval number is present; the source is a laboratory RF exposure report.
Authority or jurisdiction is inferred from CE report context and Council Recommendation 1999/519/EC; no notified body or regulatory authority is identified.
The far-field evaluation formula text appears formatting/OCR degraded, so formula details should be checked against the original PDF before reuse.
